paper_title: Spatial Correlation and Signal-to-Noise Ratio Estimation

methods.description: Method for estimating Signal-to-Noise Ratios (SNRs) of proxy records by comparing their spatial correlation structure with climate model simulations, accounting for spatial separation, time uncertainty, and additive noise. The method relies on the assumption that differences in spatial correlation between proxies and models are due to site-independent additive noise and time uncertainty.

steps.description: Identify pairs of proxy records for comparison. These can be different proxies from the same site or the same proxy from different sites.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify pairs of proxy records for comparison
inputs: list of proxy records with locations and time series
outputs: list of proxy record pairs

steps.description: Select climate model simulation data corresponding to proxy locations and timescales. Data is extracted at the nearest grid box to each proxy location.
steps.searchable_summary: Fetch climate model data for proxy locations
inputs: climate model simulation data (e.g., MPI6k, T21k)
outputs: climate model time series at proxy locations

steps.description: Define timescales for analysis: centennial (T cent) and centennial to millennial (T mill).
steps.searchable_summary: Define analysis timescales
outputs: timescale definitions (T cent, T mill)

steps.description: For T cent: Filter proxy and model time series using a cut-off frequency of 1/400 year. Remove the linear trend from the time series. Select records with a mean sampling interval less than 200 years.
steps.searchable_summary: Process time series for centennial timescale (filtering, detrending, selection)
outputs: processed time series for T cent

steps.description: For T mill: Filter proxy and model time series using a cut-off frequency of 1/1000 year. Include the linear trend in the time series. Use all records.
steps.searchable_summary: Process time series for centennial to millennial timescale (filtering, no detrending)
outputs: processed time series for T mill

steps.description: Calculate spatial correlation between pairs of processed proxy records for the defined timescales.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate spatial correlation between proxy pairs
outputs: spatial correlation values for proxy pairs

steps.description: Calculate spatial correlation between corresponding pairs of processed climate model time series for the defined timescales.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate spatial correlation between model time series pairs
inputs: spatial locations of model grid points
outputs: spatial correlation values for model pairs

steps.description: Prepare pairs of spatial correlation values from proxy records and corresponding climate model time series based on spatial separation.
steps.searchable_summary: Pair proxy and model spatial correlations by distance
inputs: spatial correlation values for proxy pairs
spatial correlation values for model pairs
outputs: paired (proxy correlation, model correlation, spatial separation) values

steps.description: Estimate SNR by matching the paired spatial correlations while accounting for time uncertainty and additive noise, quantifying SNR as a function of time uncertainty.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate SNR from paired correlations, accounting for time uncertainty and noise
inputs: paired (proxy correlation, model correlation, spatial separation) values
outputs: estimated SNR values as a function of time uncertainty

steps.description: Filter proxy pairs based on spatial separation (up to 5000 km) for the final SNR estimate.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter proxy pairs by spatial separation
outputs: filtered list of proxy pairs
filtered SNR estimates

methods.description: Process for extracting and preparing climate model simulation data for use in spatial correlation analysis and SNR estimation.

steps.description: Select climate model simulations (MPI6k, T21k) for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Select climate model simulations
outputs: selected climate model datasets (MPI6k, T21k)

steps.description: Extract annual means of surface air temperature data from the selected simulations.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract annual mean surface air temperature from climate models
outputs: annual mean temperature data from models

steps.description: Identify grid boxes in the model simulations nearest to the proxy record locations (M13, LH14, R18).
steps.searchable_summary: Find nearest model grid boxes to proxy locations
outputs: list of nearest model grid box coordinates

steps.description: Extract the annual mean temperature time series from these nearest grid boxes.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract time series from nearest model grid boxes
inputs: annual mean temperature data from models
list of nearest model grid box coordinates
outputs: climate model time series at proxy locations

methods.method_name: Spatial Correlation Structure Analysis (Model vs. Reanalysis)
methods.description: Analysis of the spatial correlation structure of climate model simulations and reanalysis data to validate the model's representation of spatial coherence, including fitting an exponential decay model.

steps.description: Obtain annual mean surface temperature field from reanalysis data (e.g., 20C3M).
steps.searchable_summary: Fetch reanalysis temperature data
outputs: reanalysis temperature field data

steps.description: Obtain annual mean surface temperature data from climate model simulations (MPI6k, T21k).
steps.searchable_summary: Fetch climate model temperature data
outputs: climate model temperature data

steps.description: Select data from both reanalysis and model simulations for specific timescales (e.g., > 400 years with trend, unfiltered annual).
steps.searchable_summary: Select data based on timescale and filtering criteria
outputs: timescale-specific temperature data

steps.description: Calculate spatial correlation (R) between time series for pairs of grid cells or locations as a function of their spatial separation (x) for the selected timescale.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate spatial correlation vs distance
inputs: temperature data (model, reanalysis) for selected timescale
outputs: pairs of (spatial separation, correlation) values for model and reanalysis

steps.description: Fit an exponential model (R = e −x/l d) to the calculated correlation-distance pairs for both model and reanalysis data.
steps.searchable_summary: Fit exponential decay model to correlation-distance data
outputs: fitted model parameters (e.g., decay length l d) for model and reanalysis

steps.description: Estimate the correlation decay length (l d) from the fitted model for each dataset (model, reanalysis) and timescale.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate correlation decay length
outputs: estimated correlation decay length (l d) for model and reanalysis

steps.description: Compare the estimated correlation decay lengths between model simulations and reanalysis data to assess model performance.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare correlation decay lengths between model and reanalysis
outputs: comparison results (e.g., difference in decay lengths)

steps.description: (Optional): Repeat steps 3-7 for different time periods of the reanalysis data (e.g., 1871-2011 vs 1871-1950) to assess sensitivity to anthropogenic forcing.
steps.searchable_summary: Repeat analysis for different reanalysis time periods
outputs: decay lengths for different reanalysis periods

steps.description: (Optional): Calculate and compare spatial correlation specifically between the proxy site locations using both model and reanalysis data.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate and compare spatial correlation at proxy sites
inputs: temperature data (model, reanalysis) at proxy locations
outputs: spatial correlation values at proxy sites (model, reanalysis)
comparison results
